varieties.name: Hyssopus officinalis (Standard Blue)
varieties.origin: Southern Europe, Mediterranean (cultivated since ancient times, 1000+ years)
varieties.traits: Upright subshrub, 50cm tall, woody base, dark green aromatic leaves 2-2.5cm long
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high, can be harvested 2-3 times per season
varieties.growing_notes: Best performer for Brisbane subtropical climate. Tolerates warm temperatures but prefers afternoon shade in peak summer. Does NOT like high humidity - plant in well-draining raised beds. Drought-tolerant once established. Prune after flowering. May struggle during humid wet summers.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Succeed Heirlooms Australia, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery

varieties.name: 'Blue Flower'
varieties.traits: Similar to standard blue, 50-60cm, compact habit
varieties.growing_notes: Selected for superior flowering. Suitable for Brisbane with good drainage and air circulation. Plant in full sun to part shade. Susceptible to fungal issues in high humidity - ensure excellent drainage.

varieties.name: 'Caeruleus'
varieties.traits: Compact plant 60cm tall, gray-green foliage, dense habit
varieties.yield_notes: Good, compact size means less space required
varieties.growing_notes: Compact size good for Brisbane gardens with limited space. Requires excellent drainage. Benefits from afternoon shade in summer. Gray-green foliage more drought-tolerant. Not ideal for prolonged humid periods.

varieties.name: 'Sissinghurst'
varieties.origin: England, named after Sissinghurst Castle Garden
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety 30cm tall, large bright green leaves, compact mounding habit
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, smaller plant = less total harvest but good leaf-to-flower ratio
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane container growing and small gardens. Lower height reduces wind damage. Good for edging. Needs very good drainage. Bright foliage more susceptible to sunburn - provide afternoon shade.
varieties.availability: Beth Chatto Gardens (UK), specialty international suppliers

varieties.name: Hyssopus officinalis f. albus (White Hyssop)
varieties.origin: Natural form, Southern Europe, cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: 50-60cm tall, dark green leaves, slightly more upright than blue form
varieties.yield_notes: Similar to standard blue variety, good leaf and flower production

varieties.name: 'Alba'
varieties.traits: Compact 60cm, dark green foliage, bushy habit
varieties.growing_notes: Selected for improved white flower form. Good for Brisbane with proper care. Plant in raised beds with excellent drainage. Benefits from organic mulch to keep roots cool in summer heat. Prune hard in spring.
varieties.availability: North Carolina Extension, specialty herb nurseries

varieties.name: Hyssopus officinalis 'Roseus'/'Rosea' (Pink Hyssop)
varieties.traits: 50-60cm tall, dark green foliage, similar habit to blue form
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good, similar to other H. officinalis varieties

varieties.name: Hyssopus officinalis subsp. aristatus (Dwarf Hyssop)
varieties.traits: Compact 30-40cm tall, smaller leaves, dense branching habit
varieties.yield_notes: Lower total yield but high density, excellent for small spaces

varieties.name: Rock Hyssop (H. officinalis cultivar)
varieties.origin: Modern cultivar selection for compact growth
varieties.traits: Low-growing 20-30cm, spreading habit, dense foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Good for size, excellent ground cover production
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for Brisbane rock gardens, raised beds, and container growing. Low profile makes it heat-tolerant and wind-resistant. Excellent drainage critical. Perfect for edging pathways or cascading over walls. Pairs well with creeping thyme. Zones 3-9.

varieties.name: Hyssopus officinalis (Standard Blue)
varieties.growing_notes: Best chance of any H. officinalis variety. Plant only during dry season (December-March). Requires raised beds 30cm high with 60% sand/gravel mix. Provide rain protection during monsoon. Expect to replace annually. Harvest before monsoon arrival.
varieties.availability: Indian Ayurvedic nurseries, Spices Board sources, Kashmir seed suppliers

varieties.name: H. officinalis f. albus (White)
varieties.growing_notes: Slightly more adaptable than blue. Same requirements as standard variety. White flowers may perform better in high elevation monsoon-edge conditions. Experimental only.
varieties.availability: Import from international suppliers, limited Indian availability

varieties.name: H. officinalis subsp. aristatus (Dwarf)
varieties.growing_notes: Mountain subspecies may tolerate high-elevation Kerala conditions better. Compact size allows for easier rain protection. Still requires dry season planting and excellent drainage. Experimental.

varieties.name: Agastache foeniculum (Anise Hyssop)
varieties.origin: Native North America (prairies), naturalized throughout US/Canada
varieties.traits: 60-120cm tall, upright habit, ovate leaves with anise-licorice scent
varieties.yield_notes: High, productive plant for both leaves and flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Performs moderately well in Brisbane with good drainage. More humidity-tolerant than Hyssopus but can get powdery mildew in wet periods. Ensure good air circulation. Self-seeds readily. Good for tea and culinary use. Zones 3-8, may struggle in hottest Brisbane summers. Plant in location with afternoon shade.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection Australia, international seed suppliers

varieties.name: 'Golden Jubilee' (A. foeniculum)
varieties.traits: 75-90cm tall, golden-yellow foliage, upright habit
varieties.growing_notes: Ornamental selection for Brisbane gardens. Golden foliage needs part shade to prevent burning. Same growing requirements as species. Unique color adds interest.

varieties.name: 'Licorice White' (A. foeniculum)
varieties.traits: 75-90cm tall, green foliage, same anise scent
varieties.growing_notes: White flowers more visible to pollinators. Good Brisbane performance with afternoon shade. Well-drained soil essential.

varieties.name: 'Alabaster' (A. foeniculum)
varieties.traits: 90cm tall, lighter green foliage, less bushy than species
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Brisbane with proper care. Lighter foliage may be more heat-sensitive - provide shade.

varieties.name: Agastache rugosa (Korean Mint)
varieties.origin: Native to East Asia (Korea, China, Japan, Vietnam, Laos)
varieties.traits: 60cm tall, glossy green leaves with purple undersides, triangular shaped
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR BRISBANE - this is the best Agastache for humid subtropical climates! Native to moist grasslands and wet environments. Tolerates humidity and wet soils much better than other species. Strong mint fragrance. Used in Korean traditional medicine. Zones 5-9. Can handle Brisbane's wet summers. Still needs good drainage but more forgiving.
varieties.availability: Asian seed suppliers, specialty herb nurseries

varieties.name: A. rugosa f. albiflora (White Korean Mint)
varieties.traits: Similar to species, green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Same excellent humidity tolerance as species. Good for Brisbane. Ornamental white form.
varieties.availability: Select Seeds, specialty Asian herb suppliers

varieties.name: 'Blue Fortune' (A. foeniculum × A. rugosa hybrid)
varieties.origin: Dutch hybrid, Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit
varieties.traits: 90cm tall, robust stems, sterile (doesn't self-seed)

varieties.name: 'Black Adder' (A. foeniculum × A. rugosa hybrid)
varieties.traits: 75cm tall, dark foliage, compact habit
varieties.yield_notes: Good, though less vigorous than 'Blue Fortune'
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Brisbane due to A. rugosa parentage. Benefits from humidity tolerance. Dramatic dark coloring. Prefers some shade in hot climate. Zones 5-9.

varieties.name: 'Little Adder' (compact A. rugosa hybrid)
varieties.traits: 45-60cm tall, dark green foliage, mounded habit
varieties.growing_notes: Compact size excellent for Brisbane container growing. Humidity-tolerant due to rugosa breeding. Good for small gardens.

varieties.name: 'Blue Blazes' (A. foeniculum hybrid)
varieties.origin: High Country Gardens introduction, A. foeniculum × 'Desert Sunrise'
varieties.traits: Tall 90-120cm, upright habit
varieties.growing_notes: Hardy to Zone 5. May tolerate Brisbane conditions with good drainage and air circulation. Attractive color combination.

varieties.name: Kudos™ Series (A. rugosa hybrids)
varieties.origin: Bred by Dan Heims at Terra Nova Nursery, Oregon
varieties.traits: Compact 45-55cm tall, bushy habit, dense branching
varieties.growing_notes: HIGHLY RECOMMENDED FOR BRISBANE! Purpose-bred for humid, wet conditions that Brisbane experiences. Most adaptable Agastache series for subtropical climates. Compact size good for containers and small gardens. Dense calyxes hold color even after flowers fade. Excellent vigor and weather tolerance.
varieties.availability: Terra Nova Nursery, High Country Gardens, premium nurseries

varieties.name: 'Heatwave' (Agastache hybrid)
varieties.traits: Upright habit, blue-green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR BRISBANE - specifically bred as 'very good performer in heat and humidity'. One of the best choices for subtropical conditions. Tolerates both heat and moisture. Well-drained soil still important.

varieties.name: Agastache mexicana (Mexican Giant Hyssop)
varieties.traits: 90cm+ tall, gray-green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Moderate Brisbane suitability - native to dry areas but can adapt. Needs excellent drainage. Best in drier months. Good hummingbird attractor (substitute: rainbow lorikeets in Brisbane).

varieties.name: 'Bolero' (A. hybrid)
varieties.traits: Large pink flower spikes, gray-green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Zones 5. Drought tolerant but grows well in regular garden conditions. Good Brisbane adaptability. Good moisture tolerance. Suitable for Brisbane with good drainage.

varieties.name: Poquito™ Series (compact hybrids)
varieties.traits: Very compact 30-40cm, perfect for containers
varieties.growing_notes: Small size makes them more manageable in Brisbane heat. Good for container growing on patios with good air flow.

varieties.name: 'Nectar Apricot'
varieties.traits: 45cm compact, apricot-orange flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Brisbane containers. Zones 7-10 - well-suited to subtropical climate.

varieties.name: 'Acapulco Deluxe Chiffon'
varieties.traits: 30cm compact, pink and orange flowers
varieties.growing_notes: Very compact, excellent for Brisbane containers and small spaces.

varieties.name: Agastache rugosa (Korean Mint)
varieties.origin: East Asia - moist grasslands, valleys, stream banks
varieties.growing_notes: BEST CHOICE for Kerala monsoon climate. Native to wet Asian environments similar to Kerala conditions. Tolerates high humidity and moisture better than any other Agastache. Plant in raised beds during dry season (December-March). May need protection from direct monsoon deluge but can handle ambient moisture. Use in herb gardens for traditional medicine (similar uses to brahmi). Harvest before peak monsoon.
varieties.availability: Can be sourced from Asian herb suppliers, Chinese/Korean medicinal herb sources

varieties.name: 'Blue Fortune' (A. foeniculum × A. rugosa)
varieties.growing_notes: Second-best choice for Kerala. Inherits A. rugosa's moisture tolerance. More ornamental than species. Requires raised beds, good drainage, but handles humidity. Plant during dry season. May succeed where true hyssop fails.

varieties.name: Kudos™ Series
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically bred for moisture tolerance. 'Kudos Ambrosia' and 'Kudos Coral' worth trying in Kerala high-elevation gardens. Compact size allows for easier management and rain protection. Experimental but promising.

varieties.name: 'Heatwave'
varieties.growing_notes: Bred for heat AND humidity tolerance. May adapt to Kerala conditions better than most. Still requires dry season planting and good drainage. Worth experimental trial.

varieties.name: Bacopa monnieri (Standard)
varieties.origin: Native to wetlands of India, Southeast Asia, Australia, globally distributed
varieties.traits: Creeping herb <30cm tall, spreading 90cm, succulent oblong leaves 4-6mm thick
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR BRISBANE! Thrives in wetland areas, bog gardens, pond edges. Perfect for Brisbane's wet conditions that challenge true hyssop. Grows in waterlogged soil or even submerged in shallow water. Can tolerate brackish conditions. Good ground cover for wet areas. Hardy Zones 8-11. Used in Ayurvedic medicine for cognitive enhancement.
varieties.availability: Native to Australia - available at aquatic plant nurseries, bog garden specialists

varieties.name: Bacopa monnieri (Kerala Native Brahmi)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to India, including Kerala - grows naturally in marshy areas throughout the region
varieties.traits: Creeping perennial herb, 15cm tall, succulent oblong leaves arranged oppositely, forms dense mat
varieties.yield_notes: High - fast-growing, can be harvested continuously. Leaves are the medicinal part.
varieties.growing_notes: IDEAL FOR KERALA! This is what should be grown instead of Hyssopus officinalis in Kerala. Perfectly adapted to monsoon climate - actually requires constant moisture. Thrives in waterlogged soil. Can be grown in: (1) Bog gardens; (2) Pond edges; (3) Paddy field borders; (4) Water gardens; (5) Hydroponic systems; (6) Containers kept constantly wet. Traditional Ayurvedic herb for memory, cognitive function, anxiety relief. Extensively used in Kerala traditional medicine.
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout Kerala - local Ayurvedic plant nurseries, traditional medicine suppliers, agricultural centers, can be collected from wild populations (with permission)
